name: European Central Bank (ECB)
summary: Day 61 Tuesday. ECB APRIL 29-30 MEETING IN <48 HOURS — HOLD AT 2.15% MOST PROBABLE; MERZ 'HUMILIATING' FRAMING IS THE EUROPEAN-LEADERSHIP TONE SHIFT TO WATCH. Per Irish Times Apr 27, German Chancellor Friedrich Merz publicly stated that Iran is 'humiliating' the US as talks stall. This is the first major European G7 leader to characterize the diplomatic deadlock in such pointed terms. Operationally significant because Germany has been the European energy-vulnerability bellwether on this crisis. Merz's characterization implies (a) European patience with the US-led blockade is finite, (b) European preference for de-escalation may override Atlantic-alliance restraint as winter-2026 storage refill begins, (c) European institutional capital (ABP, Norges Bank) may find this characterization easier to act on than mainstream Atlanticist coverage. No US response yet. Macron / Starmer have not echoed. TTF €45.17/MWh Apr 27 (+0.75% 1D, +0.5% 1M; €31 pre-crisis = €14 of crisis premium). Critically below the €70 cascade-intensity threshold (industrial demand destruction; German PMI contracts hard). ECB has NOT characterized Hormuz risk as systemic. Holds 2.15%. ECB next decision April 29-30. Stagflation framing (inflation 2.6% forecast 2026, spiking to 3.1% Q2) intact. Bond reaction Friday: 10Y 4.31% (-0.3%) range-bound. CORRECTION: yesterday's $100.47 Brent print was a yfinance contract-roll glitch; live tape Mon $108.23 / Tue $109.39 — oil REVERSED UP, not down. [TRUNCATED — needs re-synthesis]
